results.name: world-bank-trade-indicators-for-togo
results.notes:
Contains data from the World Bank's [data portal](http://data.worldbank.org/). There is also a [consolidated country dataset](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/world-bank-combined-indicators-for-togo) on HDX.

Trade is a key means to fight poverty and achieve the Millennium Development Goals, specifically by improving developing country access to markets, and supporting a rules based, predictable trading system. In cooperation with other international development partners, the World Bank launched the Transparency in Trade Initiative to provide free and easy access to data on country-specific trade policies.

organization.name: world-bank-group
organization.title: World Bank Group
organization.description: The World Bank Group is a unique global partnership comprised of five institutions working for sustainable solutions that reduce poverty and build shared prosperity in developing countries. The World Bank’s Development Data Group has a mission to provide high-quality national and international statistics to clients within and outside the Bank and to improve the capacity of member countries to produce and use statistical information. Terms of use for World Bank datasets are available on the [open data website](https://data.worldbank.org/summary-terms-of-use).

resources.name: Trade Indicators for Togo

results.name: world-bank-external-debt-indicators-for-togo
results.notes:
Contains data from the World Bank's [data portal](http://data.worldbank.org/). There is also a [consolidated country dataset](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/world-bank-combined-indicators-for-togo) on HDX.

Debt statistics provide a detailed picture of debt stocks and flows of developing countries. Data presented as part of the Quarterly External Debt Statistics takes a closer look at the external debt of high-income countries and emerging markets to enable a more complete understanding of global financial flows. The Quarterly Public Sector Debt database provides further data on public sector valuation methods, debt instruments, and clearly defined tiers of debt for central, state and local government, as well as extra-budgetary agencies and funds. Data are gathered from national statistical organizations and central banks as well as by various major multilateral institutions and World Bank staff.

organization.name: world-bank-group
organization.title: World Bank Group
organization.description: The World Bank Group is a unique global partnership comprised of five institutions working for sustainable solutions that reduce poverty and build shared prosperity in developing countries. The World Bank’s Development Data Group has a mission to provide high-quality national and international statistics to clients within and outside the Bank and to improve the capacity of member countries to produce and use statistical information. Terms of use for World Bank datasets are available on the [open data website](https://data.worldbank.org/summary-terms-of-use).

results.name: world-bank-aid-effectiveness-indicators-for-togo
results.notes:
Contains data from the World Bank's [data portal](http://data.worldbank.org/). There is also a [consolidated country dataset](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/world-bank-combined-indicators-for-togo) on HDX.

Aid effectiveness is the impact that aid has in reducing poverty and inequality, increasing growth, building capacity, and accelerating achievement of the Millennium Development Goals set by the international community. Indicators here cover aid received as well as progress in reducing poverty and improving education, health, and other measures of human welfare.

Terms of use for World Bank datasets are available on the [open data website](https://data.worldbank.org/summary-terms-of-use).

results.name: world-bank-climate-change-indicators-for-togo
results.notes:
Contains data from the World Bank's [data portal](http://data.worldbank.org/). There is also a [consolidated country dataset](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/world-bank-combined-indicators-for-togo) on HDX.

Climate change is expected to hit developing countries the hardest. Its effects—higher temperatures, changes in precipitation patterns, rising sea levels, and more frequent weather-related disasters—pose risks for agriculture, food, and water supplies. At stake are recent gains in the fight against poverty, hunger and disease, and the lives and livelihoods of billions of people in developing countries. Addressing climate change requires unprecedented global cooperation across borders. The World Bank Group is helping support developing countries and contributing to a global solution, while tailoring our approach to the differing needs of developing country partners.  Data here cover climate systems, exposure to climate impacts, resilience, greenhouse gas emissions, and energy use.  Other indicators relevant to climate change are found under other data pages, particularly Environment, Agriculture & Rural Development, Energy & Mining, Health, Infrastructure, Poverty, and Urban Development.

organization.name: world-bank-group
organization.title: World Bank Group
organization.description: The World Bank Group is a unique global partnership comprised of five institutions working for sustainable solutions that reduce poverty and build shared prosperity in developing countries. The World Bank’s Development Data Group has a mission to provide high-quality national and international statistics to clients within and outside the Bank and to improve the capacity of member countries to produce and use statistical information. Terms of use for World Bank datasets are available on the [open data website](https://data.worldbank.org/summary-terms-of-use).

results.name: world-bank-economy-and-growth-indicators-for-togo
results.notes:
Contains data from the World Bank's [data portal](http://data.worldbank.org/). There is also a [consolidated country dataset](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/world-bank-combined-indicators-for-togo) on HDX.

Economic growth is central to economic development. When national income grows, real people benefit. While there is no known formula for stimulating economic growth, data can help policy-makers better understand their countries' economic situations and guide any work toward improvement. Data here covers measures of economic growth, such as gross domestic product (GDP) and gross national income (GNI). It also includes indicators representing factors known to be relevant to economic growth, such as capital stock, employment, investment, savings, consumption, government spending, imports, and exports.

Terms of use for World Bank datasets are available on the [open data website](https://data.worldbank.org/summary-terms-of-use).

results.name: who-data-for-tgo
results.notes:
This dataset contains data from WHO's [data portal](https://www.who.int/gho/en/) covering the following categories:  
  
Air pollution, Child mortality, Dementia diagnosis, treatment and care, Environment and health, Food safety, Global Dementia Observatory (GDO), Global Health Estimates: Life expectancy and leading causes of death and disability, Global Information System on Alcohol and Health, Global Patient Safety Observatory, HIV, Health financing, Health systems, Health taxes, Health workforce, Hepatitis, Immunization coverage and vaccine-preventable diseases, Malaria, Maternal and reproductive health, Mental health, Neglected tropical diseases, Noncommunicable diseases, Nutrition, Oral Health, Priority health technologies, Resources for Substance Use Disorders, Road Safety, Sexually Transmitted Infections, Substance use disorders service coverage, Tobacco control, Tuberculosis, Universal health coverage (UHC), SDG Target 3.8, Vaccine-preventable communicable diseases, Violence prevention, Water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH), World Health Statistics.  
  
For links to individual indicator metadata, see resource descriptions.

organization.name: world-health-organization
organization.title: World Health Organization
organization.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) is the United Nations agency that connects nations, partners and people to promote health, keep the world safe and serve the vulnerable – so everyone, everywhere can attain the highest level of health.

The Organization leads global efforts to expand universal health coverage. It directs and coordinates the world’s response to health emergencies. It promotes healthier lives – from pregnancy care through old age. The Triple Billion targets outline an ambitious plan for the world to achieve good health for all using science-based policies and programmes.

resources.description:
*Malaria:*
[Estimated number of malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-number-of-malaria-cases), [Estimated number of malaria deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-number-of-malaria-deaths), [Number of confirmed malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-confirmed-malaria-cases), [Estimated malaria incidence (per 1000 population at risk)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/malaria-incidence-%28per-1-000-population-at-risk%29), [Estimated malaria mortality rate (per 100 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-malaria-mortality-rate-per-100-000-population), [Number of imported malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-imported-malaria-cases), [Number of indigenous malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-indigenous-malaria-cases), [Number of malaria positive cases by microscopy](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-malaria-positive-cases-by-microscopy), [Number of malaria suspects examined by microscopy](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-malaria-suspects-examined-by-microscopy), [Number of indigenous P. falciparum malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-indigenous-p.-falciparum-malaria-cases), [Number of presumed malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-presumed-malaria-cases), [Number of indigenous P. vivax malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-indigenous-p.-vivax-malaria-cases), [Number of malaria positive cases by rapid diagnostic test (RDT)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-malaria-positive-cases-by-rapid-diagnostic-test-rdt), [Number of suspected malaria cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-suspected-malaria-cases), [Total number of  malaria cases (presumed + confirmed cases)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-number-of-malaria-cases-presumed-confirmed-cases)

rhodesiense)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-new-reported-cases-of-human-african-trypanosomiasis-%28t-b-rhodesiense%29), [Status of elimination of *Child mortality:*
[Number of deaths among children 5 to 14 years of age](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-among-children-ages-5-to-14-years), [Mortality rate for 5-14 year-olds (probability of dying per 1000 children aged 5-14 years)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mortality-rate-for-5-14-year-olds-%28probability-of-dying-per-1000-children-aged-5-14-years%29), [Number of under-five deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-under-five-deaths), [Number of infant deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-infant-deaths), [Number of neonatal deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-neonatal-deaths), [Number of deaths among children ages 5 to 9 years](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-among-children-ages-5-to-9-years), [Number of deaths among adolescents (10 to 19 years of age)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-among-adolescents-10-19-years-of-age), [Infant mortality rate (probability of dying between birth and age 1 per 1000 live births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/infant-mortality-rate-%28probability-of-dying-between-birth-and-age-1-per-1000-live-births%29), [Under-five mortality rate (probability of dying by age 5 per 1000 live births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/under-five-mortality-rate-%28probability-of-dying-by-age-5-per-1000-live-births%29), [Adolescent mortality rate (per 1 000 age specific cohort)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/adolescent-mortality-rate-per-1000-age-specific-cohort), [Number of deaths in children aged <5 years, by cause](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths), [Deaths per 1 000 live births](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/deaths-per-1-000-live-births), [Distribution of causes of death among children aged < 5 years (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/distribution-of-causes-of-death-among-children-aged-5-years-%28-%29), [Neonatal mortality rate (per 1000 live births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/neonatal-mortality-rate-%28per-1000-live-births%29), [Stillbirth rate (per 1000 total births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/stillbirth-rate-%28per-1000-total-births%29), [Mortality rate among children ages 5 to 9 years (per 1000 children aged 5)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mortality-rate-among-children-ages-5-to-9-years-%28per-1000-children-aged-5%29)

*Maternal and reproductive health:*

resources.description:
*Global Health Estimates: Life expectancy and leading causes of death and disability:*
[Number of deaths among children 5 to 14 years of age](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-among-children-ages-5-to-14-years), [Mortality rate for 5-14 year-olds (probability of dying per 1000 children aged 5-14 years)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mortality-rate-for-5-14-year-olds-%28probability-of-dying-per-1000-children-aged-5-14-years%29), [Number of under-five deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-under-five-deaths), [Number of infant deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-infant-deaths), [Number of neonatal deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-neonatal-deaths), [Number of deaths among children ages 5 to 9 years](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-among-children-ages-5-to-9-years), [Number of deaths among adolescents (10 to 19 years of age)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-among-adolescents-10-19-years-of-age), [Infant mortality rate (probability of dying between birth and age 1 per 1000 live births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/infant-mortality-rate-%28probability-of-dying-between-birth-and-age-1-per-1000-live-births%29), [Under-five mortality rate (probability of dying by age 5 per 1000 live births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/under-five-mortality-rate-%28probability-of-dying-by-age-5-per-1000-live-births%29), [Age-standardized suicide rates (per 100 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/age-standardized-suicide-rates-%28per-100-000-population%29), [Adolescent mortality rate (per 1 000 age specific cohort)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/adolescent-mortality-rate-per-1000-age-specific-cohort), [Number of deaths in children aged <5 years, by cause](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths), [Deaths per 1 000 live births](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/deaths-per-1-000-live-births), [Distribution of causes of death among children aged < 5 years (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/distribution-of-causes-of-death-among-children-aged-5-years-%28-%29), [Number of maternal deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-maternal-deaths), [Probability (%) of dying between age 30 and exact age 70 from any of cardiovascular disease, cancer, diabetes, or chronic respiratory disease](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/probability-of-dying-between-exact-ages-30-and-70-from-any-of-cardiovascular-disease-cancer-diabetes-or-chronic-respiratory-%28-%29), [Total NCD Deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/gho-ghe-ncd-deaths-in-thousands), [Premature deaths due to noncommunicable diseases (NCD) as a proportion of all NCD deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/ncd-deaths-under-age-70-%28percent-of-all-ncd-deaths%29), [Estimated road traffic death rate (per 100 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-road-traffic-death-rate-%28per-100-000-population%29), [Distribution of road traffic deaths by type of road user (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/distribution-of-road-traffic-deaths-by-type-of-road-user-%28-%29), [Mortality rate attributed to unintentional poisoning (per 100 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mortality-rate-attributed-to-unintentional-poisoning-%28per-100-000-population%29), [Crude suicide rates (per 100 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/crude-suicide-rates-%28per-100-000-population%29), [Life expectancy at birth (years)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/life-expectancy-at-birth-%28years%29), [Healthy life expectancy (HALE) at birth (years)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/gho-ghe-hale-healthy-life-expectancy-at-birth), [Neonatal mortality rate (per 1000 live births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/neonatal-mortality-rate-%28per-1000-live-births%29), [Adult mortality rate (probability of dying between 15 and 60 years per 1000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/adult-mortality-rate-%28probability-of-dying-between-15-and-60-years-per-1000-population%29), [Healthy life expectancy (HALE) at age 60 (years)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/gho-ghe-hale-healthy-life-expectancy-at-age-60), [Stillbirth rate (per 1000 total births)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/stillbirth-rate-%28per-1000-total-births%29), [Life expectancy at age 60 (years)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/life-expectancy-at-age-60-%28years%29), [Mortality rate among children ages 5 to 9 years (per 1000 children aged 5)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mortality-rate-among-children-ages-5-to-9-years-%28per-1000-children-aged-5%29)

resources.description:
*Global Information System on Alcohol and Health:*
[Alcohol, recorded per capita (15+) consumption (in litres of pure alcohol), by beverage type](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-recorded-per-capita-%2815-%29-consumption-%28in-litres-of-pure-alcohol%29), [Alcohol, drinkers only per capita (15+) consumption in litres of pure alcohol, three-year average](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-drinkers-only-per-capita-%2815-%29consumption-in-litres-of-pure-alcohol), [Alcohol, abstainers lifetime (%), age-standardized](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-abstainers-lifetime-%28-%29), [Alcohol, abstainers past 12 months (%), age-standardized](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-abstainers-past-12-months-%28-%29), [Alcohol, consumers past 12 months (%), age-standardized](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-consumers-past-12-months-%28-%29), [Alcohol, former drinkers (%), age-standardized](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-former-drinkers-%28-%29), [Awareness activities](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/awareness-activities), [Alcohol, total per capita (15+) consumption (in litres of pure alcohol) (SDG Indicator 3.5.2), three-year average](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-%28recorded-unrecorded%29-alcohol-per-capita-%2815-%29-consumption), [Alcohol, heavy episodic drinking (15+) past 30 days (%), age-standardized](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-heavy-episodic-drinking-%2815-%29-past-30-days-%28-%29-age-standardized-with-95-ci), [Alcohol, recorded per capita (15+) consumption (in litres of pure alcohol), three-year average](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-recorded-per-capita-%2815-%29-consumption-%28in-litres-of-pure-alcohol%29-3-year-average-with-95-ci), [Alcohol, average daily intake in grams among drinkers, three-year average](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/alcohol-average-daily-intake-in-grams-among-drinkers-with-95-ci), [15-19 years old, current drinkers (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/15-19-years-old-current-drinkers-%28-%29), [National guidelines for the prevention and reduction of alcohol-related harm in schools](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/national-guidelines-for-the-prevention-and-reduction-of-alcohol-related-harm-in-schools), [National guidelines for alcohol problem prevention and counselling at workplaces](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/national-guidelines-for-alcohol-problem-prevention-and-counselling-at-workplaces), [Server training](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/server-training), [Legislation on alcohol testing at workplaces](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/legislation-on-alcohol-testing-at-workplaces), [Standard drink defined](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/standard-drink-defined), [National systems for monitoring alcohol consumption and harms](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/national-systems-for-monitoring-alcohol-consumption-and-harms), [National surveys on adult alcohol consumption](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/national-surveys-on-adult-alcohol-consumption), [National surveys on youth alcohol consumption](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/national-surveys-on-youth-alcohol-consumption), [National system of epidemiological data collection for alcohol resources.description:
*HIV:*
[Estimated number of people (all ages) living with HIV](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-number-of-people--living-with-hiv), [Number of people dying from HIV-related causes](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-deaths-due-to-hiv-aids), [Testing and counselling facilities, reported number](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hiv-testing-and-counselling---testing-and-counselling-facilities), [Testing and counselling facilities, estimated number per 100 000 adult population](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hiv-testing-and-counselling---testing-and-counselling-facilities), [Reported number of people receiving antiretroviral therapy](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/reported-number-of-people-receiving-antiretroviral-therapy), [Reported number of children receiving antiretroviral therapy](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/reported-number-of-children-receiving-antiretroviral-therapy), [Number of pregnant women living with HIV who received antiretrovirals for preventing mother-to-child transmission](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-pregnant-women-living-with-hiv-who-received-antiretrovirals-for-preventing-mother-to-child-transmission), [Estimated number of pregnant women living with HIV](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-percentage-of-pregnant-women-living-with-hiv-who-received-antiretrovirals-for-preventing-mother-to-child-transmission), [Estimated number of pregnant women living with HIV needing antiretrovirals for preventing mother-to-child transmission](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-number-of-pregnant-women-living-with-hiv-needing-antiretrovirals-for-preventing-mother-to-child-transmission), [Estimated number of children needing antiretroviral therapy based on WHO methods](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-number-of-children-needing-antiretroviral-therapy-based-on-who-methods), [Estimated antiretroviral therapy coverage among children](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-antiretroviral-therapy-coverage-among-children), [Testing and counselling facilities, reporting period](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hiv-testing-and-counselling---testing-and-counselling-facilities), [Number of new HIV infections](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-new-hiv-infections), [Estimated antiretroviral therapy coverage among people living with HIV (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/estimated-antiretroviral-therapy-coverage-among-people-living-with-hiv-%28-%29), [Percentage of HIV-positive results returned to people in the calendar year](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hiv-testing-volume-and-positivity), [Prevalence of HIV among adults aged 15 to 49 (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/prevalence-of-hiv-among-adults-aged-15-to-49-%28-%29), [New HIV infections (per 1000 uninfected population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/new-hiv-infections-%28per-1000-uninfected-population%29)

(CHE) per capita in US$](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/current-health-expenditure-%28che%29-per-capita-in-us-dollar), [External health expenditure (EXT) as percentage of current health expenditure (CHE) (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/external-health-expenditure-%28ext%29-as-percentage-of-current-health-expenditure-%28che%29-%28-%29), [External health (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/domestic-general-government-health-expenditure-%28gche-d%29-as-percentage-of-current-health-expenditure), [Domestic general government health expenditure (GGHE-D) as percentage of gross domestic product (GDP) [Domestic general government health expenditure (GGHE-D) per capita in (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hepatitis--diagnosis-coverage-of-chronic-hepatitis-%28hbv-and-hcv%29-as-a-percentage-of-infected), [New chronic hepatitis B [Dentists (per 10,000)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/dentists-%28per-10-000-population%29), [Dentists (number)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/dentists-%28number%29), [Dental Assistants and Therapists (number)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/dental-assistants-and-therapists-%28number%29), [Dental Prosthetic Technicians (number)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/dental-prosthetic-technicians-%28number%29), [Affordability of fluoride toothpaste](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/affordability-of-fluoride-toothpaste), [Per capita expenditure on dental healthcare (US$)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/per-capita-expenditure-on-dental-healthcare-%28us-dollar%29), [Total expenditure on dental healthcare in million (US$)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-expenditure-on-dental-healthcare-in-million-%28us-dollar%29), [Prevalence of edentulism in people 20+ years (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/prevalence-of-edentulism-in-people-20--years), [Number of labour days needed to buy annual supply of fluoride toothpaste per person](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-labour-days-needed-to-buy-annual-supply-of-fluoride-toothpaste-per-person), [Dental prosthetic technicians (per 10 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/oral-health--dental-prosthetic-technicians), [Presence of dedicated staff for oral health working on NCDs at the MoH](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/presence-of-dedicated-staff-for-oral-health-working-on-ncds-at-the-moh), [Oral health: Noma recognized as a national public resources.description:
*Priority health technologies:*
[Availability of national standards or recommended lists of medical devices](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/availability-of-national-standards-or-recommended-lists-of-medical-devices), [Types of lists recommending health technology for high burden diseases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/types-of-lists-recommending-health-technology-for-high-burden-diseases), [Total density per million population: Magnetic Resonance Imaging](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-population-magnetic-resonance-imaging), [Total density per million population: Computed tomography units](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-population-computed-tomography-units), [Total density per million population: Positron Emission tomography](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-population-positron-emission-tomography), [Total density per million population: Linear Accelerator](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-population-linear-accelerator), [Use of nomenclature system](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/use-of-nomenclature-system), [Health technology (medical device) national policy](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/health-technology-%28medical-device%29-national-policy), [Unit in the Ministry of Health responsible for the management of medical devices](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/unit-in-the-ministry-of-health-responsible-for-the-management-of-medical-devices), [Procurement of medical devices carried out at the national level](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/procurement-of-medical-devices-carried-out-at-the-national-level), [Availability of national list of approved medical devices for procurement or reimbursement](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/availability-of-national-list-of-approved-medical-devices-for-procurement-or-reimbursement), [National guidelines, policies or recommendations on the procurement of medical devices](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/national-guidelines-policies-or-recommendations-on-the-procurement-of-medical-devices), [Availability of technical specifications of medical devices to support procurement or donations](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/availability-of-technical-specifications-of-medical-devices-to-support-procurement-or-donations), [Total density per million population: Telecobalt Unit](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-population-telecobalt-unit), [Total density per million population: Radiotherapy units](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-population-radiotherapy-units), [Total density per million females aged from 50 to 69 years old: Mammography units](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-density-per-million-females-aged-from-50-to-69-years-old-mammography-units), [Biomedical engineers density (per 10 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/biomedical-engineers-density-%28per-10-000-population%29), [Number of biomedical engineers](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-biomedical-engineers), [Biomedical technicians density (per 10 000 population)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/biomedical-technicians-density-%28per-10-000-population%29), [Number of biomedical technicians](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-biomedical-technicians)

resources.description:
*Resources for Substance Use Disorders:*
[Government unit/official responsible for prevention for substance use](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/government-unit-official-responsible-for-prevention-for-substance-use), [Ministry/office that takes primary responsibility for prevention for substance use](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/ministry-office-that-takes-primary-responsibility-for-prevention-for-substance-use), [Five years change in international cooperation for prevention for substance use](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/five-years-change-in-international-cooperation-for-prevention-for-substance-use), [Government unit/official responsible for treatment of substance use disorders](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/government-unit-official-responsible-for-treatment-of-substance-use-disorders), [Ministry/office that takes primary responsibility for treatment of substance use disorders](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/ministry-office-that-takes-primary-responsibility-for-treatment-of-substance-use-disorders), [Involvement of representatives of affected or targeted populations in the development and formulation of policies and strategies for treatment of substance use disorders](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/involvement-of-representatives-of-affected-or-targeted-populations-in-the-development-and-formulation-of-policies-and-strategies-for-treatment-of-substance-use-disorders), [Involvement of representatives of affected or targeted populations in the development and implementation of national programmes for treatment of substance use disorders](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/involvement-of-representatives-of-affected-or-targeted-populations-in-the-development-and-implementation-of-national-programmes-for-treatment-of-substance-use-disorders), [Involvement of representatives of affected or targeted populations in the development and formulation of policies and strategies for prevention for alcohol and drugs](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/involvement-of-representatives-of-affected-or-targeted-populations-in-the-development-and-formulation-of-policies-and-strategies-for-prevention-for-alcohol-and-drugs), [Involvement of representatives of affected or targeted populations in the development and implementation of national programmes for prevention for alcohol and drugs](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/involvement-of-representatives-of-affected-or-targeted-populations-in-the-development-and-implementation-of-national-programmes-for-prevention-for-alcohol-and-drugs), [Five years change in international cooperation for treatment of substance use *Road Safety:*
[Existence of a national drink-driving law](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/existence-of-a-national-drink-driving-law), [Definition of drink-driving by BAC](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/definition-of-drink-driving-by-bac), [Blood Alcohol Concentration (BAC) limit for drivers](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/blood-alcohol-concentration-%28bac%29-limit-for-drivers), [Attribution of road traffic deaths to alcohol (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/attribution-of-road-traffic-deaths-to-alcohol-%28-%29), [Existence of a national seat-belt law](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/existence-of-a-national-seat-belt-law), [Applicability of seat-belt law to all occupants](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/applicability-of-seat-belt-law-to-all-occupants), [Seat-belt wearing rate (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/seat-belt-wearing-rate-%28-%29), [Existence of a national child-restraint law](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/existence-of-a-national-child-restraint-law), [Existence of national speed limits](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/existence-of-national-speed-limits), [Maximum speed limits](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/maximum-speed-limits), [Applicability of national motorcycle helmet law to all occupants](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/applicability-of-national-motorcycle-helmet-law-to-all-occupants), [Law requires helmet to be fastened](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/law-requires-helmet-to-be-fastened), [Existence of a road safety lead agency](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/existence-of-a-road-safety-lead-agency), [Existence of a national road safety strategy](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/existence-of-a-national-road-safety-strategy), [Availability of funding for national road safety *Sexually Transmitted Infections:*
[Congenital syphilis number of cases, reported](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/congenital-syphilis-number-of-reported-cases), [Antenatal care attendees who were positive for syphilis (%), reported](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/antenatal-care-attendees-who-were-positive-for-syphilis-%28-%29), [Women accessing antenatal care (ANC) services who were tested for syphilis (%), reported](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/women-accessing-antenatal-care-%28anc%29-services-who-were-tested-for-syphilis-%28-%29), [Antenatal care attendees positive for syphilis who received treatment (%), reported](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/antenatal-care-attendees-positive-for-syphilis-who-received-treatment-%28-%29), [Congenital syphilis rate per 100 000 live births, reported](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/congenital-syphilis-rate-per-100-000-live-births), [Incident rate of active syphilis (per 1000)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/incident-rate-of-active-syphilis-%28per-1000%29), [Incident cases of active syphilis in 15-49 year olds (in thousands)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/incident-cases-of-syphilis-in-individuals-%28in-millions%29), [Prevalence of active syphilis in 15-49 year olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/prevalence-of-active-syphilis-in-individuals-%28-%29), [Prevalent cases of active syphilis (in thousands)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/prevalent-cases-of-active-syphilis-%28in-millions%29)

advertising](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/tobacco-enforce-bans--e2-indirect-advertising), [Tobacco Enforce bans: E3 indirect advertising, additional bans](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/tobacco-enforce-bans--e3-indirect-advertising--additional-bans), [Tobacco Monitor: population-based national surveys, tobacco use and smoking](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/monitor--m1-population-based-national-surveys--use-and-smoking), [Tobacco Monitor: population-based national surveys, smokeless tobacco or e-cigarettes](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/monitor--m2-population-based-national-surveys--smokeless-or-e-cigarettes), [Tobacco Monitor: school-based national surveys, tobacco use, tobacco smoking, and cigarette tobacco dependence / Availability of smoking cessation support](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/offer-help--o1-support-for-treatment-of-dependence), [Tobacco Offer resources.description:
*Vaccine-preventable communicable diseases:*
[Proportion of vaccination cards seen (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/gho-immunization-vaccination-cards-seen), [Measles-containing-vaccine second-dose (MCV2) immunization coverage by the nationally recommended age (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/measles-containing-vaccine-second-dose-%28mcv2%29-immunization-coverage-by-the-nationally-recommended-age-%28-%29), [Rotavirus vaccines completed dose (RotaC) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/rotavirus-vaccines-completed-dose-%28rotac%29-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-%28-%29), [HPV immunization coverage estimates among primary target cohort (9-14 years old girls) (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/girls-aged-15-years-old-that-received-the-recommended-doses-of-hpv-vaccine), [Meningococcal A conjugate (MENGA) vaccine immunization coverage (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/vaccine-coverage-mena-a-meningitiscoverage), [Yellow fever vaccine (YFV) immunization coverage among 1-year olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/gho-vaccinecoverage-yfv-yellowfevervaccinecoverage), [Diphtheria - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/diphtheria---number-of-reported-cases), [Japanese encephalitis - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/japanese-encephalitis---number-of-reported-cases), [Pertussis - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/pertussis-number-of-reported-cases), [Total tetanus - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/total-tetanus---number-of-reported-cases), [Poliomyelitis - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/poliomyelitis---number-of-reported-cases), [Yellow fever - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/yellow-fever-number-of-reported-cases), [Mumps - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mumps---number-of-reported-cases), [Congenital Rubella Syndrome - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/congenital-rubella-syndrome-number-of-reported-cases), [Neonatal tetanus - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/neonatal-tetanus---number-of-reported-cases), [Rubella - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/rubella---number-of-reported-cases), [Measles - number of reported cases](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/measles---number-of-reported-cases), [Hepatitis B (HepB3) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hepatitis-b-%28hepb3%29-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-%28-%29), [Neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus (PAB) (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/neonates-protected-at-birth-against-neonatal-tetanus-%28pab%29-%28-%29), [Hib (Hib3) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/hib-hib3-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds), [BCG immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/bcg-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-%28-%29), [Polio (Pol3) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/polio-%28pol3%29-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-%28-%29), [Measles-containing-vaccine first-dose (MCV1) immunization coverage among 1-year-olds (%)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/measles-containing-vaccine-first-dose-%28mcv1%29-immunization-coverage-among-1-year-olds-%28-%29)

*Water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH):*
[Amount of water- and sanitation-related official development assistance that is part of a government-coordinated spending plan (constant 2024 US$ millions)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/amount-of-water--and-sanitation-related-official-development-assistance-that-is-part-of-a-government-coordinated-spending-plan), [Mortality rate attributed to exposure to unsafe WASH services (per 100 000 population) (SDG 3.9.2)](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/mortality-rate-attributed-to-exposure-to-unsafe-wash-services-%28per-100-000-population%29-%28sdg-3-9-2%29), [SDG 3.9.2 WASH deaths](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/sdg-3-9-2-wash-deaths), [Number of diarrhoea deaths from inadequate water, sanitation and hygiene](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-diarrhoea-deaths-from-inadequate-water-sanitation-and-hygiene), [Number of diarrhoea deaths from inadequate hygiene](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-diarrhoea-deaths-from-inadequate-hygiene), [Number of diarrhoea deaths from inadequate sanitation](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-diarrhoea-deaths-from-inadequate-sanitation), [Number of diarrhoea deaths from inadequate water](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/number-of-diarrhoea-deaths-from-inadequate-water), [Attributable fraction of diarrhoea to inadequate water, sanitation and hygiene](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/attributable-fraction-of-diarrhoea-to-inadequate-water-sanitation-and-hygiene), [Attributable fraction of diarrhoea to inadequate hygiene](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/attributable-fraction-of-diarrhoea-to-inadequate-hygiene), [Attributable fraction of diarrhoea to inadequate water](https://www.who.int/data/gho/data/indicators/indicator-details/GHO/attributable-fraction-of-diarrhoea-to-inadequate-water), [Number of diarrhoea DALYs from inadequate water, sanitation and results.notes:
This dataset contains data from WHO's [data portal](https://www.who.int/gho/en/) covering the following categories:  
  
Air pollution, Antimicrobial resistance (AMR), Assistive technology, Child mortality, Dementia diagnosis, treatment and care, Dementia policy and legislation, Environment and health, Foodborne Diseases Estimates, Global Dementia Observatory (GDO), Global Health Estimates: Life expectancy and leading causes of death and disability, Global Information System on Alcohol and Health, HIV, Health Inequality Monitor, Health financing, Health systems, Health taxes, Health workforce, Hepatitis, Immunization coverage and vaccine-preventable diseases, International Health Regulations (2005) monitoring framework, Malaria, Maternal and reproductive health, Mental health, Neglected tropical diseases, Noncommunicable diseases, Nutrition, Oral Health, Priority health technologies, Resources for Substance Use Disorders, Road Safety, SDG Target 3.8 | Achieve universal health coverage (UHC), Sexually Transmitted Infections, Tobacco control, Tuberculosis, Vaccine-preventable communicable diseases, Violence against women, Violence prevention, Water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH), Women and health, World Health Statistics.  
  
For links to individual indicator metadata, see resource descriptions.

organization.name: world-health-organization
organization.title: World Health Organization
organization.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) is the United Nations agency that connects nations, partners and people to promote health, keep the world safe and serve the vulnerable – so everyone, everywhere can attain the highest level of health.

The Organization leads global efforts to expand universal health coverage. It directs and coordinates the world’s response to health emergencies. It promotes healthier lives – from pregnancy care through old age. The Triple Billion targets outline an ambitious plan for the world to achieve good health for all using science-based policies and programmes.

resources.name: World Health Statistics Indicators for Togo

results.methodology_other: Twice a year in over 90 countries, the Meta Survey Team sends the Future of Business to admins and owners of Facebook-designated small business pages. When we share data from this survey, we anonymize responses to all survey questions and only share country-level data publicly. To achieve better representation of the broader small business population, we also weight our results based on known characteristics of the Facebook Page admin population.
results.name: future-of-business-survey-aggregated-data
results.notes:
More than 200 million businesses use Facebook globally. The goal of our Small Business Surveys has been to learn about the unique perspectives, challenges and opportunities of small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). Through 2022, the Future of Business Survey and the Global State of Small Business (GSoSB) Survey were conducted in partnership with the World Bank and Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development.


Aggregated country level data for each survey wave is available to the public on HDX and controlled access microdata is available to Data for Good at Meta partners. Please visit https://ai.meta.com/ai-for-good/datasets/small-business-surveys/ to apply for access to microdata or contact aiforgood@meta.com for any questions.

This dataset regularly updated until 2023-03-24 and is no longer expecting additional data.

When data is shared responsibly with the communities that need it, it can improve wellbeing and save lives.
